# @zakkster/lite-headless / empty-state

Structural primitive for the "no items" / "no results" / "error" /
"loading-but-not-yet" UI pattern that recurs across admin products.
Owns ARIA wiring (live region + labelledby + describedby chain) and
slot-marker detection; consumer owns layout, icon, and action buttons.

## What you get

```js
import { createEmptyState } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/empty-state";

const es = createEmptyState({ variant: "empty" });
es.attachRoot(rootEl);
es.attachIcon(iconEl);
es.attachTitle(titleEl);
es.attachDescription(descEl);
es.attachActions(actionsEl);

// Variant switching (e.g. when an async fetch errors out):
es.setVariant("error");
```

## Variants (the `variant` enum)

Three canonical kinds. The variant is painted as `data-variant="<v>"`
on the root; consumer CSS branches off it.

- `"empty"` — list/container has no items (the typical case)
- `"error"` — async load failed
- `"loading"` — pre-data placeholder (a step beyond skeleton — when
  you have copy explaining what's coming)

Custom variant strings are allowed; they just become the painted enum.

## Painted attributes

Root (`[data-empty-state-root]`):
- `role="status"` + `aria-live="polite"` (live region; new content is announced)
- `aria-labelledby` → title id (when title attached)
- `aria-describedby` → description id (when description attached)
- `data-variant="empty|error|loading|..."`
- `data-empty` (boolean; for variant-agnostic CSS)

Title (`[data-empty-title]`):
- Falls back to `role="heading"` + `aria-level="2"` if the element is
  not already an `<h1>`–`<h4>`. Use a real `<h2>` or `<h3>` and the
  primitive leaves it alone.
- Gets a generated id if it doesn't have one (for the labelledby link).

Description (`[data-empty-description]`):
- Gets a generated id (for the describedby link).

Icon (`[data-empty-icon]`):
- `aria-hidden="true"` (icons here are decorative).

Actions (`[data-empty-actions]`):
- `role="group"` (announced as a CTA cluster).

## Wrapper (`<lite-empty-state>`)

```html
<lite-empty-state variant="empty">
    <div data-empty-icon>📭</div>
    <h3 data-empty-title>No projects yet</h3>
    <p data-empty-description>Create one to start tracking work.</p>
    <div data-empty-actions>
        <button>Create project</button>
        <button>Import</button>
    </div>
</lite-empty-state>
```

Reactive attributes:
- `variant="empty|error|loading|..."` — bidirectional; external
  setAttribute("variant", "error") flows through `setVariant`.

Host accessors:
- `host.variant` (current variant string)
- `host.setVariant(v)`
- `host._emptyStateInstance` (advanced)

Late-injected slot markers are auto-discovered via the wrapper's
`createRoleObserver`; the consumer can swap title/desc/icon at runtime
and the ARIA chain re-links itself.

## Composition

The variant axis makes one wrapper handle multiple UI states. A common
pattern is to let the data layer drive the variant:

```js
effect(() => {
    if      (fetcher.isError())   host.setVariant("error");
    else if (fetcher.isLoading()) host.setVariant("loading");
    else if (fetcher.data().length === 0) host.setVariant("empty");
    else host.style.display = "none";    // not empty; hide the placeholder
});
```

The CSS branches show / hide the relevant copy per variant:

```css
[data-variant="error"] [data-empty-title]:not(.t-error) { display: none; }
[data-variant="loading"] [data-empty-title]:not(.t-loading) { display: none; }
[data-variant="empty"] [data-empty-title]:not(.t-empty) { display: none; }
```

## CSS contract

Follows the canonical CSS contract in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`.
`data-empty` is the Class-2 boolean marker; `data-variant` is a
Class-3 enum. Slot markers (`data-empty-icon`, `data-empty-title`,
etc.) are Class-4 inputs.
